perturbation.original_text: "SPONSOR WEB SITE" means that Web Site, the primary home page for which is located at URL http://www.ual.com and any successor or replacement thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: "SPONSOR WEB SITE" means that online portal, the primary home page for which is located at URL http://www.ual.com and any successor or replacement thereof.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Web Site' is changed to 'online portal.' This creates confusion because 'Web Site' is a defined term used consistently throughout the agreement, whereas 'online portal' is not. This may lead to disputes about whether sections that refer to the 'Sponsor Web Site' also include other online presences of the Sponsor. The contradiction arises in sections discussing the scope of access and usage rights granted concerning the Sponsor Web Site, specifically regarding what constitutes the 'Sponsor Web Site' for purposes of content display and linking.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to Section 2.4 [APPROVAL OF TRADEMARK USAGE], Sponsor hereby grants Snap a non-exclusive, revocable nontransferable, royalty-free, worldwide license to: (a) use, reproduce, publish, perform and display the Sponsor Marks and Sponsor Brand Features on the Co-Branded Pages; (b) link to the Sponsor Web Site from the Co-Branded Pages and/or the Snap Web Site that contains the Co-Branded Pages and (c) use, reproduce, publish, perform, and display the Sponsor Content in and on the Co-Branded Pages. All goodwill arising out of Snap's use of any of the Sponsor Marks shall inure solely to the benefit of Sponsor.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Web Site' to 'online portal' creates a strong in-text contradiction because 'Web Site' is a defined term used throughout the agreement, including in the Contradicted Text which grants rights to use the Sponsor Marks and link to the 'Sponsor Web Site'. By changing the definition to 'online portal', the scope of these granted rights becomes unclear. It introduces ambiguity regarding whether the license to link to the 'Sponsor Web Site' still applies, given the change in terminology. This creates uncertainty and a potential conflict, especially regarding content display and linking, as the Contradicted Text refers directly to the 'Sponsor Web Site'. This misalignment of terminology directly contradicts the defined scope of access and usage rights related to the Sponsor Web Site, potentially impacting the enforceability of the license granted in the Contradicted Text. This follows the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Following the execution of this Agreement, Snap and Sponsor will work in good faith to develop the content specified in Exhibit C. All content to be included on the Co-Branded Pages shall be required to comply with Snap's generally applicable content and technical guidelines.
perturbation.changed_text: Following the execution of this Agreement, Snap and Sponsor will work in good faith to develop the content specified in Exhibit C. All content to be included on the Co-Branded Pages shall be required to comply with Snap's internal content policies.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'Snap's generally applicable content and technical guidelines' is changed to 'Snap's internal content policies.' This introduces ambiguity, as 'internal content policies' is not a defined term and may or may not encompass the same scope as the 'generally applicable content and technical guidelines.' This change creates a contradiction in the enforceability of content standards, especially if the 'internal content policies' are less clearly defined or accessible than the previously referenced guidelines. It becomes unclear what standards the Sponsor's content must meet, creating a conflict regarding content obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Following the execution of this Agreement, Snap and Sponsor will work in good faith to develop the Co-Branded Pages which may include all or part of the CO-BRANDING AGREEMENT PAGE 2 SNAP CONFIDENTIAL * Portions of this exhibit have been omitted and filed separately with the Commission pursuant to a request for confidential treatment under Rule 406. Sponsor Content and/or the Snap Content. The Co-Branded Pages will have the specifications set forth on Exhibit C. The Co-Branded Pages shall be required to comply with Snap's generally applicable content and technical guidelines. Sponsor will supply Sponsor Content and Sponsor Brand Features as agreed upon by the parties for inclusion in the Co-Branded Pages. Following the development thereof, Snap shall use commercially reasonable efforts to post and maintain the Co-Branded Pages on the Snap Web Site. Snap may from time to time, in consultation with Sponsor, update and modify the Co-Branded Pages but may not change Sponsor content or Sponsor Brand Features, or do anything else with them, without Sponsor's prior written consent.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Snap's generally applicable content and technical guidelines' with 'Snap's internal content policies.' The original text and contradicted text both reference compliance with 'Snap's generally applicable content and technical guidelines.' By changing this phrase to the undefined 'Snap's internal content policies,' the modification introduces ambiguity, creating a contradiction regarding the content obligations and the standards Sponsor's content must meet. Since this modifies a key term which appears in the contradicted section, it's a strong contradiction under the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In the event of termination or expiration of this Agreement for other than a material breach of this Agreement by Sponsor, upon notice from Sponsor delivered to Snap at least forty-five (45) days prior to such expiration or termination, Snap shall negotiate in good faith an agreement providing Sponsor with sponsorship rights similar to those described herein on terms and conditions to be mutually agreed upon by the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of termination or expiration of this Agreement for other than a significant violation of this Agreement by Sponsor, upon notice from Sponsor delivered to Snap at least forty-five (45) days prior to such expiration or termination, Snap shall negotiate in good faith an agreement providing Sponsor with sponsorship rights similar to those described herein on terms and conditions to be mutually agreed upon by the parties.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'material breach' is changed to 'significant violation'. 'Material breach' is a legal standard with established case law, while 'significant violation' is vague. This introduces uncertainty. The contradictory effect is centered around the triggering event for Snap's obligation to negotiate in good faith for renewed sponsorship rights. A dispute could arise over whether a given breach qualifies as a 'significant violation,' especially if it would have clearly been considered a 'material breach.' This ambiguity undercuts the enforceability of the right of first refusal.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event of termination or expiration of this Agreement for other than a significant violation of this Agreement by Sponsor, upon notice from Sponsor delivered to Snap at least forty-five (45) days prior to such expiration or termination, Snap shall negotiate in good faith an agreement providing Sponsor with sponsorship rights similar to those described herein on terms and conditions to be mutually agreed upon by the parties. In the event that an agreement CO-BRANDING AGREEMENT PAGE 4 SNAP CONFIDENTIAL * Portions of this exhibit have been omitted and filed separately with the Commission pursuant to a request for confidential treatment under Rule 406. between the parties is not executed within thirty (30) days following delivery, of such notice to Snap, Snap shall be free thereafter to enter into such an agreement with any third party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'material breach' with 'significant violation.' Although 'significant violation' is arguably more vague, both the changed text and the contradicted text contain the exact same phrasing: 'significant violation of this Agreement.' There is no direct contradiction here; both excerpts rely on this same ambiguous term. The explanation argues that the change *introduces* uncertainty, which is true, but that doesn't automatically mean there's an in-text contradiction according to the defined parameters. Because both clauses now contain the same ambiguous terminology, there is no change in obligation or enforcement.
